text: The estimated treatment difference in body weight was -9.6 percentage points (95% CI, -11.1 to -8.2) with semaglutide 2.4mg versus placebo.
The difference in weight change was -10.3 percentage points (95% CI, -12.0 to -8.6; P<0.001) with semaglutide plus intensive behavioral therapy.
The estimated treatment difference was -14.8 percentage points (95% CI, -16.0 to -13.5) comparing continued semaglutide to switched placebo.
The difference in body weight change was -12.8 percentage points (95% CI, -14.0 to -11.5) with tirzepatide 15mg.
The difference in weight reduction was -18.4 percentage points (95% CI, -21.1 to -15.7) with tirzepatide after intensive lifestyle intervention.
The difference in body weight was -17.9 percentage points (95% CI, -19.3 to -16.5) comparing continued tirzepatide to switched placebo.
The estimated treatment difference in body weight was -12.0 percentage points (95% CI, -13.6 to -10.4; P<0.001) with oral semaglutide 50mg.
The difference in HbA1c was -0.54 percentage points (95% CI, -0.71 to -0.37) with dulaglutide 4.5mg versus 1.5mg.
The estimated treatment difference in HbA1c was -1.1 percentage points (95% CI, -1.3 to -0.9) with oral semaglutide 14mg.
The estimated treatment difference in HbA1c was -0.5 percentage points (95% CI, -0.7 to -0.4) with oral semaglutide versus empagliflozin.
The difference in HbA1c was -1.91 percentage points (95% CI, -2.09 to -1.73) with tirzepatide 15mg.
The difference in HbA1c from baseline was -0.45 percentage points (95% CI, -0.57 to -0.33; P<0.001) with tirzepatide versus semaglutide.
The difference in HbA1c was -0.93 percentage points (95% CI, -1.10 to -0.76; P<0.001) with tirzepatide 15mg versus insulin degludec.
The difference in CDR-SB at week 78 was -0.39 (95% CI, -0.69 to -0.09; P=0.01) with high-dose aducanumab.
